1→"""All enums and ORM models for the claims processing system.
     2→
     3→Models are defined in FK-dependency order so SQLAlchemy can resolve
     4→relationships without forward-reference strings:
     5→Member → Provider → Plan → CoverageRule → Policy → Accumulator →
     6→Claim → ClaimStatusHistory → AdjudicationResult → LineItem → Dispute → Payment
     7→"""
     8→
     9→import enum
    10→import uuid
    11→from datetime import date, datetime
    12→from decimal import Decimal
    13→
    14→from sqlalchemy import (
    15→    Boolean,
    16→    CheckConstraint,
    17→    Date,
    18→    DateTime,
    19→    Enum,
    20→    ForeignKey,
    21→    Index,
    22→    Integer,
    23→    Numeric,
    24→    String,
    25→    Text,
    26→    UniqueConstraint,
    27→    text,
    28→)
    29→from sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship
    30→
    31→from .extensions import Base
    32→
    33→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34→# Enums
    35→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    36→
    37→
    38→class ProviderType(enum.Enum):
    39→    individual = "individual"
    40→    facility = "facility"
    41→
    42→
    43→class PolicyStatus(enum.Enum):
    44→    active = "active"
    45→    expired = "expired"
    46→    cancelled = "cancelled"
    47→
    48→
    49→class ClaimStatus(enum.Enum):
    50→    submitted = "submitted"
    51→    under_review = "under_review"
    52→    approved = "approved"
    53→    denied = "denied"
    54→    partially_approved = "partially_approved"
    55→    paid = "paid"
    56→
    57→
    58→class ReviewType(enum.Enum):
    59→    auto = "auto"
    60→    manual = "manual"
    61→
    62→
    63→class LineItemStatus(enum.Enum):
    64→    pending = "pending"
    65→    approved = "approved"
    66→    denied = "denied"
    67→
    68→
    69→class DisputeStatus(enum.Enum):
    70→    pending = "pending"
    71→    resolved = "resolved"
    72→
    73→
    74→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→# Models
    76→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77→
    78→
    79→class Member(Base):
    80→    """An individual insured person."""
    81→
    82→    __tablename__ = "members"
    83→
    84→    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    85→    name: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)
    86→    date_of_birth: Mapped[date] = mapped_column(Date, nullable=False)
    87→    email: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), unique=True, nullable=False)
    88→    phone: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(50), nullable=True)
    89→    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow)
    90→    deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=True)
    91→
    92→    policies: Mapped[list["Policy"]] = relationship("Policy", back_populates="member")
    93→    claims: Mapped[list["Claim"]] = relationship("Claim", back_populates="member")
    94→    accumulators: Mapped[list["Accumulator"]] = relationship("Accumulator", back_populates="member")
    95→
    96→
    97→class Provider(Base):
    98→    """A healthcare professional or facility."""
    99→
   100→    __tablename__ = "providers"
   101→
   102→    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   103→    name: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)
   104→    npi: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(20), unique=True, nullable=False)
   105→    provider_type: Mapped[ProviderType] = mapped_column(Enum(ProviderType), nullable=False)
   106→    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow)
   107→    deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=True)
   108→
   109→
   110→class Plan(Base):
   111→    """Benefit structure template: coverage rules and deductible amount."""
   112→
   113→    __tablename__ = "plans"
   114→    __table_args__ = (CheckConstraint("deductible >= 0", name="ck_plans_deductible_non_negative"),)
   115→
   116→    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   117→    name: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)
   118→    deductible: Mapped[Decimal] = mapped_column(Numeric(10, 2), nullable=False)
   119→    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow)
   120→    deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=True)
   121→
   122→    coverage_rules: Mapped[list["CoverageRule"]] = relationship(
   123→        "CoverageRule", back_populates="plan"
   124→    )
   125→    policies: Mapped[list["Policy"]] = relationship("Policy", back_populates="plan")
   126→
   127→
   128→class CoverageRule(Base):
   129→    """Coverage definition for a single CPT code on a plan.
   130→
   131→    The (plan_id, cpt_code) uniqueness constraint for active rules is enforced
   132→    at the service layer because SQLite lacks partial indexes without triggers.
   133→    """
   134→
   135→    __tablename__ = "coverage_rules"
   136→    __table_args__ = (
   137→        CheckConstraint(
   138→            "coverage_percentage >= 0 AND coverage_percentage <= 1",
   139→            name="ck_coverage_rules_percentage_range",
   140→        ),
   141→    )
   142→
   143→    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   144→    plan_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), ForeignKey("plans.id"), nullable=False)
   145→    cpt_code: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(20), nullable=False)
   146→    is_covered: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, nullable=False)
   147→    coverage_percentage: Mapped[Decimal] = mapped_column(Numeric(5, 4), nullable=False)
   148→    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow)
   149→    deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=True)
   150→
   151→    plan: Mapped[Plan] = relationship("Plan", back_populates="coverage_rules")
   152→
   153→
   154→class Policy(Base):
   155→    """A member's active instance of a plan for a specific policy period."""
   156→
   157→    __tablename__ = "policies"
   158→    __table_args__ = (
   159→        CheckConstraint("end_date >= start_date", name="ck_policies_end_after_start"),
   160→        Index(
   161→            "ix_policies_member_id_active",
   162→            "member_id",
   163→            unique=True,
   164→            sqlite_where=text("status = 'active' AND deleted_at IS NULL"),
   165→        ),
   166→    )
   167→
   168→    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   169→    member_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), ForeignKey("members.id"), nullable=False)
   170→    plan_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), ForeignKey("plans.id"), nullable=False)
   171→    start_date: Mapped[date] = mapped_column(Date, nullable=False)
   172→    end_date: Mapped[date] = mapped_column(Date, nullable=False)
   173→    status: Mapped[PolicyStatus] = mapped_column(
   174→        Enum(PolicyStatus), nullable=False, default=PolicyStatus.active
   175→    )
   176→    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow)
   177→    deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=True)
   178→
   179→    member: Mapped[Member] = relationship("Member", back_populates="policies")
   180→    plan: Mapped[Plan] = relationship("Plan", back_populates="policies")
   181→    claims: Mapped[list["Claim"]] = relationship("Claim", back_populates="policy")
   182→    accumulator: Mapped["Accumulator | None"] = relationship(
   183→        "Accumulator", back_populates="policy", uselist=False
   184→    )
   185→
   186→
   187→class Accumulator(Base):
   188→    """Running deductible total per member per policy period."""
   189→
   190→    __tablename__ = "accumulators"
   191→    __table_args__ = (
   192→        UniqueConstraint("member_id", "policy_id", name="uq_accumulator_member_policy"),
   193→        CheckConstraint("deductible_met >= 0", name="ck_accumulators_deductible_met_non_negative"),
   194→    )
   195→
   196→    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   197→    member_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), ForeignKey("members.id"), nullable=False)
   198→    policy_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(
   199→        String(36), ForeignKey("policies.id"), unique=True, nullable=False
   200→    )
   201→    deductible_met: Mapped[Decimal] = mapped_column(
   202→        Numeric(10, 2), nullable=False, default=Decimal("0.00")
   203→    )
   204→    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow)
   205→    updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
   206→        DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow, onupdate=datetime.utcnow
   207→    )
   208→    deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=True)
   209→
   210→    member: Mapped[Member] = relationship("Member", back_populates="accumulators")
   211→    policy: Mapped[Policy] = relationship("Policy", back_populates="accumulator")
   212→
   213→
   214→class Claim(Base):
   215→    """A reimbursement request submitted by or on behalf of a member."""
   216→
   217→    __tablename__ = "claims"
   218→
   219→    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   220→    member_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), ForeignKey("members.id"), nullable=False)
   221→    policy_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), ForeignKey("policies.id"), nullable=False)
   222→    provider_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), ForeignKey("providers.id"), nullable=False)
   223→    date_of_service: Mapped[date] = mapped_column(Date, nullable=False)
   224→    status: Mapped[ClaimStatus] = mapped_column(
   225→        Enum(ClaimStatus), nullable=False, default=ClaimStatus.submitted
   226→    )
   227→    review_type: Mapped[ReviewType] = mapped_column(
   228→        Enum(ReviewType), nullable=False, default=ReviewType.auto
   229→    )
   230→    submitted_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
   231→        DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow
   232→    )
   233→    updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
   234→        DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow, onupdate=datetime.utcnow
   235→    )
   236→    deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=True)
   237→
   238→    member: Mapped[Member] = relationship("Member", back_populates="claims")
   239→    policy: Mapped[Policy] = relationship("Policy", back_populates="claims")
   240→    provider: Mapped[Provider] = relationship("Provider")
   241→    line_items: Mapped[list["LineItem"]] = relationship(
   242→        "LineItem",
   243→        back_populates="claim",
   244→        foreign_keys="LineItem.claim_id",
   245→    )
   246→    status_history: Mapped[list["ClaimStatusHistory"]] = relationship(
   247→        "ClaimStatusHistory", back_populates="claim", order_by="ClaimStatusHistory.transitioned_at"
   248→    )
   249→    dispute: Mapped["Dispute | None"] = relationship(
   250→        "Dispute", back_populates="claim", uselist=False
   251→    )
   252→    payment: Mapped["Payment | None"] = relationship(
   253→        "Payment", back_populates="claim", uselist=False
   254→    )
   255→
   256→
   257→class ClaimStatusHistory(Base):
   258→    """Append-only audit log of claim status transitions."""
   259→
   260→    __tablename__ = "claim_status_history"
   261→
   262→    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   263→    claim_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), ForeignKey("claims.id"), nullable=False)
   264→    from_status: Mapped[ClaimStatus | None] = mapped_column(Enum(ClaimStatus), nullable=True)
   265→    to_status: Mapped[ClaimStatus] = mapped_column(Enum(ClaimStatus), nullable=False)
   266→    transitioned_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
   267→        DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow
   268→    )
   269→    note: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)
   270→
   271→    claim: Mapped[Claim] = relationship("Claim", back_populates="status_history")
   272→
   273→
   274→class AdjudicationResult(Base):
   275→    """Immutable record of a single adjudication pass on a line item.
   276→
   277→    Re-adjudication appends a new row (revision + 1) rather than mutating.
   278→    """
   279→
   280→    __tablename__ = "adjudication_results"
   281→    __table_args__ = (
   282→        UniqueConstraint("line_item_id", "revision", name="uq_adjudication_line_revision"),
   283→        CheckConstraint("revision >= 1", name="ck_adjudication_results_revision_min"),
   284→        CheckConstraint(
   285→            "applied_to_deductible >= 0",
   286→            name="ck_adjudication_results_applied_non_negative",
   287→        ),
   288→        CheckConstraint("plan_pays >= 0", name="ck_adjudication_results_plan_pays_non_negative"),
   289→        CheckConstraint(
   290→            "member_owes >= 0", name="ck_adjudication_results_member_owes_non_negative"
   291→        ),
   292→    )
   293→
   294→    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   295→    line_item_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(
   296→        String(36), ForeignKey("line_items.id"), nullable=False
   297→    )
   298→    revision: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, nullable=False)
   299→    is_covered: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, nullable=False)
   300→    applied_to_deductible: Mapped[Decimal] = mapped_column(Numeric(10, 2), nullable=False)
   301→    plan_pays: Mapped[Decimal] = mapped_column(Numeric(10, 2), nullable=False)
   302→    member_owes: Mapped[Decimal] = mapped_column(Numeric(10, 2), nullable=False)
   303→    explanation: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=False)
   304→    adjudicated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
   305→        DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow
   306→    )
   307→
   308→    line_item: Mapped["LineItem"] = relationship(
   309→        "LineItem", back_populates="results", foreign_keys=[line_item_id]
   310→    )
   311→
   312→
   313→class LineItem(Base):
   314→    """A single billable service within a claim.
   315→
   316→    ``latest_result_id`` uses ``use_alter=True`` to resolve the mutual FK
   317→    cycle with ``AdjudicationResult`` (SQLAlchemy emits it as a post-creation
   318→    ALTER TABLE statement).
   319→    """
   320→
   321→    __tablename__ = "line_items"
   322→    __table_args__ = (
   323→        CheckConstraint("billed_amount > 0", name="ck_line_items_billed_amount_positive"),
   324→    )
   325→
   326→    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   327→    claim_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), ForeignKey("claims.id"), nullable=False)
   328→    diagnosis_code: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(20), nullable=False)
   329→    cpt_code: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(20), nullable=False)
   330→    billed_amount: Mapped[Decimal] = mapped_column(Numeric(10, 2), nullable=False)
   331→    adjudication_status: Mapped[LineItemStatus] = mapped_column(
   332→        Enum(LineItemStatus), nullable=False, default=LineItemStatus.pending
   333→    )
   334→    latest_result_id: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(
   335→        String(36),
   336→        ForeignKey("adjudication_results.id", use_alter=True, name="fk_line_item_latest_result"),
   337→        nullable=True,
   338→    )
   339→    updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
   340→        DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow, onupdate=datetime.utcnow
   341→    )
   342→    deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime, nullable=True)
   343→
   344→    claim: Mapped[Claim] = relationship(
   345→        "Claim", back_populates="line_items", foreign_keys=[claim_id]
   346→    )
   347→    results: Mapped[list[AdjudicationResult]] = relationship(
   348→        "AdjudicationResult",
   349→        back_populates="line_item",
   350→        foreign_keys="AdjudicationResult.line_item_id",
   351→        order_by="AdjudicationResult.revision",
   352→    )
   353→    latest_result: Mapped[AdjudicationResult | None] = relationship(
   354→        "AdjudicationResult",
   355→        foreign_keys=[latest_result_id],
   356→        primaryjoin="LineItem.latest_result_id == AdjudicationResult.id",
   357→    )
   358→
   359→
   360→class Dispute(Base):
   361→    """A member's challenge to a denied or partially-approved claim.
   362→
   363→    One dispute per claim maximum, enforced by the unique FK constraint.
   364→    """
   365→
   366→    __tablename__ = "disputes"
   367→
   368→    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   369→    claim_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(
   370→        String(36), ForeignKey("claims.id"), unique=True, nullable=False
   371→    )
   372→    reason: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=False)
   373→    reviewer_note: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)
   374→    submitted_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(
   375→        DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow
   376→    )

1→"""Dispute service — submit and manage member disputes on claims."""
     2→
     3→import logging
     4→from datetime import datetime
     5→from decimal import Decimal
     6→
     7→from app.errors import ConflictError, NotFoundError
     8→from app.extensions import db
     9→from app.models import (
    10→    Claim,
    11→    ClaimStatus,
    12→    ClaimStatusHistory,
    13→    Dispute,
    14→    DisputeStatus,
    15→    LineItemStatus,
    16→    Payment,
    17→    ReviewType,
    18→)
    19→
    20→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    21→
    22→
    23→def submit_dispute(claim_id: str, reason: str) -> Dispute:
    24→    """Submit a dispute for a denied or partially-approved claim.
    25→
    26→    Transitions the claim to ``under_review`` with ``review_type=manual`` and
    27→    creates a ``Dispute`` row with ``status=pending``.
    28→
    29→    Args:
    30→        claim_id: UUID string of the claim to dispute.
    31→        reason: The member's reason for disputing the claim.
    32→
    33→    Returns:
    34→        The newly created Dispute.
    35→
    36→    Raises:
    37→        NotFoundError: If no claim with *claim_id* exists.
    38→        ConflictError: If the claim status does not allow a dispute (not
    39→            ``denied`` or ``partially_approved``), or if the claim has already
    40→            been disputed.
    41→    """
    42→    claim: Claim | None = db.session.get(Claim, claim_id)
    43→    if claim is None:
    44→        raise NotFoundError(f"Claim {claim_id!r} not found")
    45→
    46→    if claim.status not in (ClaimStatus.denied, ClaimStatus.partially_approved):
    47→        raise ConflictError(
    48→            f"Cannot dispute a claim with status '{claim.status.value}'. "
    49→            "Only 'denied' or 'partially_approved' claims may be disputed."
    50→        )
    51→
    52→    if claim.dispute is not None:
    53→        raise ConflictError("This claim has already been disputed and cannot be disputed again.")
    54→
    55→    old_status = claim.status
    56→    claim.status = ClaimStatus.under_review
    57→    claim.review_type = ReviewType.manual
    58→
    59→    db.session.add(
    60→        ClaimStatusHistory(
    61→            claim_id=claim.id,
    62→            from_status=old_status,
    63→            to_status=ClaimStatus.under_review,
    64→            note="Dispute submitted by member.",
    65→        )
    66→    )
    67→
    68→    dispute = Dispute(claim_id=claim.id, reason=reason)
    69→    db.session.add(dispute)
    70→    db.session.commit()
    71→
    72→    logger.info(
    73→        "claim %s dispute submitted; transitioned %s → under_review",
    74→        claim.id,
    75→        old_status.value,
    76→    )
    77→    return dispute
    78→
    79→
    80→def trigger_readjudication(claim_id: str, reviewer_note: str | None) -> Claim:
    81→    """Resolve a pending dispute and trigger re-adjudication.
    82→
    83→    Marks the dispute as resolved, then delegates to AdjudicationEngine which
    84→    commits the entire transaction.  The engine auto-pays if the re-adjudication
    85→    result is ``partially_approved`` (dispute already resolved, no further
    86→    dispute is possible).
    87→
    88→    Args:
    89→        claim_id: UUID string of the claim to re-adjudicate.
    90→        reviewer_note: Optional note from the reviewer; stored on the Dispute.
    91→
    92→    Returns:
    93→        The refreshed Claim with its new final status.
    94→
    95→    Raises:
    96→        ConflictError: If the claim is not ``under_review`` with
    97→            ``review_type=manual``, or if the dispute is already resolved.
    98→        NotFoundError: If no claim or no dispute exists for *claim_id*.
    99→    """
   100→    claim: Claim | None = db.session.get(Claim, claim_id)
   101→    if claim is None:
   102→        raise NotFoundError(f"Claim {claim_id!r} not found")
   103→
   104→    if claim.status != ClaimStatus.under_review or claim.review_type != ReviewType.manual:
   105→        raise ConflictError("Claim is not awaiting manual review")
   106→
   107→    dispute: Dispute | None = claim.dispute
   108→    if dispute is None:
   109→        raise NotFoundError(f"No dispute found for claim {claim_id!r}")
   110→    if dispute.status != DisputeStatus.pending:
   111→        raise ConflictError("Dispute is already resolved")
   112→
   113→    if reviewer_note is not None:
   114→        dispute.reviewer_note = reviewer_note
   115→    dispute.status = DisputeStatus.resolved
   116→    dispute.resolved_at = datetime.utcnow()
   117→    db.session.flush()
   118→
   119→    # Import here to avoid circular imports at module load time
   120→    from app.services.adjudication_engine import AdjudicationEngine
   121→
   122→    AdjudicationEngine().run(claim.id)
   123→
   124→    db.session.refresh(claim)
   125→    logger.info("claim %s re-adjudicated; final status=%s", claim.id, claim.status.value)
   126→    return claim
   127→
   128→
   129→def accept_payment(claim_id: str) -> Payment:
   130→    """Accept partial payment for a partially-approved claim (no active dispute).
   131→
   132→    Creates a Payment row for the sum of plan_pays on approved line items and
   133→    transitions the claim to ``paid``.
   134→
   135→    Args:
   136→        claim_id: UUID string of the claim.
   137→
   138→    Returns:
   139→        The newly created Payment.
   140→
   141→    Raises:
   142→        ConflictError: If the claim is not ``partially_approved``, or if a
   143→            Dispute row already exists for this claim.
   144→        NotFoundError: If no claim exists for *claim_id*.
   145→    """
   146→    claim: Claim | None = db.session.get(Claim, claim_id)
   147→    if claim is None:
   148→        raise NotFoundError(f"Claim {claim_id!r} not found")
   149→
   150→    if claim.status != ClaimStatus.partially_approved:
   151→        raise ConflictError(f"Cannot accept payment for a claim with status '{claim.status.value}'")
   152→
   153→    if claim.dispute is not None:
   154→        raise ConflictError("Dispute already filed; cannot accept partial payment")
   155→
   156→    approved_pays: Decimal = sum(  # type: ignore[assignment]
   157→        li.latest_result.plan_pays
   158→        for li in claim.line_items
   159→        if li.deleted_at is None
   160→        and li.adjudication_status == LineItemStatus.approved
   161→        and li.latest_result is not None
   162→    )
   163→
   164→    payment = Payment(claim_id=claim.id, amount=approved_pays)
   165→    db.session.add(payment)
   166→
   167→    old_status = claim.status
   168→    claim.status = ClaimStatus.paid
   169→    db.session.add(
   170→        ClaimStatusHistory(
   171→            claim_id=claim.id,
   172→            from_status=old_status,
   173→            to_status=ClaimStatus.paid,
   174→        )
   175→    )
   176→    db.session.commit()
   177→
   178→    logger.info("claim %s accepted partial payment; amount=%s", claim.id, approved_pays)
   179→    return payment
   180→

   240→
   241→    Returns:
   242→        200 with payment data, or 409 on error.
   243→    """
   244→    payment = dispute_service.accept_payment(claim_id)
   245→    return jsonify(_serialize_payment(payment))
   246→
   247→
   248→@bp.route("/<claim_id>/payment", methods=["GET"])
   249→def get_payment(claim_id: str) -> Response:
   250→    """Retrieve the payment record for a claim.
   251→
   252→    Args:
   253→        claim_id: UUID of the claim.
   254→
   255→    Returns:
   256→        200 with payment data, or 404 if claim or payment not found.
   257→    """
   258→    claim: Claim | None = db.session.execute(
   259→        db.select(Claim).where(Claim.id == claim_id, Claim.deleted_at.is_(None))
   260→    ).scalar_one_or_none()
   261→    if claim is None:
   262→        raise NotFoundError(f"Claim '{claim_id}' not found")
   263→    if claim.payment is None:
   264→        raise NotFoundError(f"No payment found for claim '{claim_id}'")
   265→    return jsonify(_serialize_payment(claim.payment))

content.text: Now let me check the adjudication engine to understand how re-adjudication works:

content.content:
1→"""Adjudication engine — evaluates claim line items against plan coverage rules.
     2→
     3→All DB writes in ``AdjudicationEngine.run`` occur inside a single transaction
     4→that is committed at the end of the method.  No other service should call
     5→``db.session.commit()`` while the engine is running.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import logging
     9→from decimal import ROUND_HALF_UP, Decimal
    10→
    11→from sqlalchemy import select
    12→
    13→from app.extensions import db
    14→from app.models import (
    15→    Accumulator,
    16→    AdjudicationResult,
    17→    Claim,
    18→    ClaimStatus,
    19→    ClaimStatusHistory,
    20→    CoverageRule,
    21→    Dispute,
    22→    DisputeStatus,
    23→    LineItemStatus,
    24→    Payment,
    25→)
    26→
    27→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    28→
    29→
    30→class AdjudicationEngine:
    31→    """Evaluate a claim's line items and drive it to a final status.
    32→
    33→    The engine is stateless; instantiate a new one per adjudication run.
    34→    """
    35→
    36→    def run(self, claim_id: str) -> Claim:
    37→        """Adjudicate all active line items on *claim_id* and commit the result.
    38→
    39→        Steps (all in one transaction):
    40→        1. Load the Accumulator for the claim's policy.
    41→        2. Transition ``submitted → under_review`` (skip if already ``under_review``).
    42→        3. For each active LineItem (ordered by id):
    43→           - Reset adjudication_status + latest_result_id.
    44→           - Look up CoverageRule; derive approved/denied result.
    45→           - INSERT AdjudicationResult; flush to get its id.
    46→           - Update LineItem.latest_result_id / adjudication_status.
    47→        4. Update Accumulator.deductible_met.
    48→        5. Derive ClaimStatus from line item outcomes.
    49→        6. Transition ``under_review → <final_status>``.
    50→        7. If ``approved``: transition to ``paid``; create Payment when plan_pays > 0.
    51→        8. If ``partially_approved`` AND a resolved Dispute exists: create Payment,
    52→           transition to ``paid``.
    53→        9. Commit.
    54→
    55→        Args:
    56→            claim_id: UUID string of the claim to adjudicate.
    57→
    58→        Returns:
    59→            The refreshed Claim with its final status and updated relationships.
    60→
    61→        Raises:
    62→            ValueError: If the claim does not exist.
    63→            RuntimeError: If the policy has no Accumulator (data-integrity error).
    64→        """
    65→        claim = db.session.get(Claim, claim_id)
    66→        if claim is None:
    67→            raise ValueError(f"Claim {claim_id!r} not found")
    68→
    69→        # 1. Load accumulator
    70→        accumulator: Accumulator | None = db.session.scalar(
    71→            select(Accumulator).where(Accumulator.policy_id == claim.policy_id)
    72→        )
    73→        if accumulator is None:
    74→            raise RuntimeError(
    75→                f"No Accumulator found for policy {claim.policy_id!r} — "
    76→                "policy was not created correctly"
    77→            )
    78→
    79→        plan = claim.policy.plan
    80→
    81→        # 2. Transition submitted → under_review (idempotent for re-adjudication)
    82→        if claim.status == ClaimStatus.submitted:
    83→            self._transition(claim, ClaimStatus.submitted, ClaimStatus.under_review)
    84→
    85→        # 3. Process each active line item in deterministic order
    86→        active_items = sorted(
    87→            (li for li in claim.line_items if li.deleted_at is None),
    88→            key=lambda li: li.id,
    89→        )
    90→        total_consumed: Decimal = Decimal("0.00")
    91→
    92→        for line_item in active_items:
    93→            # Reset for (re-)adjudication
    94→            line_item.adjudication_status = LineItemStatus.pending
    95→            line_item.latest_result_id = None
    96→
    97→            cpt_code = line_item.cpt_code
    98→            billed = line_item.billed_amount
    99→
   100→            # Look up coverage rule — raises MultipleResultsFound if duplicate active rules exist
   101→            rule: CoverageRule | None = db.session.scalar(
   102→                select(CoverageRule).where(
   103→                    CoverageRule.plan_id == plan.id,
   104→                    CoverageRule.cpt_code == cpt_code,
   105→                    CoverageRule.deleted_at.is_(None),
   106→                )
   107→            )
   108→
   109→            revision = max((r.revision for r in line_item.results), default=0) + 1
   110→
   111→            if rule is None or not rule.is_covered:
   112→                result = AdjudicationResult(
   113→                    line_item_id=line_item.id,
   114→                    revision=revision,
   115→                    is_covered=False,
   116→                    applied_to_deductible=Decimal("0.00"),
   117→                    plan_pays=Decimal("0.00"),
   118→                    member_owes=billed,
   119→                    explanation=f"Service {cpt_code} is not covered under your plan.",
   120→                )
   121→                line_item.adjudication_status = LineItemStatus.denied
   122→            else:
   123→                remaining = plan.deductible - accumulator.deductible_met - total_consumed
   124→                applied_to_deductible = min(billed, max(Decimal("0.00"), remaining))
   125→                amount_after_deductible = billed - applied_to_deductible
   126→                coverage_pct: Decimal = rule.coverage_percentage
   127→                plan_pays = (amount_after_deductible * coverage_pct).quantize(
   128→                    Decimal("0.01"), ROUND_HALF_UP
   129→                )
   130→                member_owes = (billed - plan_pays).quantize(Decimal("0.01"), ROUND_HALF_UP)
   131→                total_consumed += applied_to_deductible
   132→
   133→                pct_display = float(coverage_pct) * 100
   134→                explanation = (
   135→                    f"Service {cpt_code} covered at {pct_display:.4g}%. "
   136→                    f"${applied_to_deductible} applied to deductible. "
   137→                    f"Plan pays ${plan_pays}; you owe ${member_owes}."
   138→                )
   139→                result = AdjudicationResult(
   140→                    line_item_id=line_item.id,
   141→                    revision=revision,
   142→                    is_covered=True,
   143→                    applied_to_deductible=applied_to_deductible,
   144→                    plan_pays=plan_pays,
   145→                    member_owes=member_owes,
   146→                    explanation=explanation,
   147→                )
   148→                line_item.adjudication_status = LineItemStatus.approved
   149→
   150→            db.session.add(result)
   151→            db.session.flush()  # get result.id
   152→
   153→            line_item.latest_result_id = result.id
   154→            logger.info(
   155→                "claim %s line_item %s cpt=%s covered=%s applied_deductible=%s plan_pays=%s",
   156→                claim.id,
   157→                line_item.id,
   158→                cpt_code,
   159→                result.is_covered,
   160→                result.applied_to_deductible,
   161→                result.plan_pays,
   162→            )
   163→
   164→        # 4. Update accumulator
   165→        accumulator.deductible_met += total_consumed
   166→
   167→        # 5. Derive claim-level status from line item outcomes
   168→        statuses = {li.adjudication_status for li in active_items}
   169→        if statuses == {LineItemStatus.approved}:
   170→            final_status = ClaimStatus.approved
   171→        elif statuses == {LineItemStatus.denied}:
   172→            final_status = ClaimStatus.denied
   173→        else:
   174→            final_status = ClaimStatus.partially_approved
   175→
   176→        # 6. Transition under_review → final status
   177→        self._transition(claim, ClaimStatus.under_review, final_status)
   178→
   179→        # 7. approved → paid  (Payment only when plan actually pays something)
   180→        if final_status == ClaimStatus.approved:
   181→            self._transition(claim, ClaimStatus.approved, ClaimStatus.paid)
   182→            total_plan_pays: Decimal = sum(  # type: ignore[assignment]
   183→                li.latest_result.plan_pays for li in active_items if li.latest_result is not None
   184→            )
   185→            if total_plan_pays > Decimal("0.00"):
   186→                db.session.add(Payment(claim_id=claim.id, amount=total_plan_pays))
   187→
   188→        # 8. partially_approved + resolved dispute → paid
   189→        elif final_status == ClaimStatus.partially_approved:
   190→            dispute: Dispute | None = claim.dispute
   191→            if dispute is not None and dispute.status == DisputeStatus.resolved:
   192→                approved_pays: Decimal = sum(  # type: ignore[assignment]
   193→                    li.latest_result.plan_pays
   194→                    for li in active_items
   195→                    if li.adjudication_status == LineItemStatus.approved
   196→                    and li.latest_result is not None
   197→                )
   198→                self._transition(claim, ClaimStatus.partially_approved, ClaimStatus.paid)
   199→                db.session.add(Payment(claim_id=claim.id, amount=approved_pays))
   200→
   201→        # 9. Commit
   202→        db.session.commit()
   203→        db.session.refresh(claim)
   204→        return claim
   205→
   206→    def _transition(
   207→        self,
   208→        claim: Claim,
   209→        from_status: ClaimStatus,
   210→        to_status: ClaimStatus,
   211→    ) -> None:
   212→        """Update claim status and append a ClaimStatusHistory row.
   213→
   214→        Args:
   215→            claim: The claim to transition.
   216→            from_status: The status being left.
   217→            to_status: The status being entered.
   218→        """
   219→        claim.status = to_status
   220→        db.session.add(
   221→            ClaimStatusHistory(
   222→                claim_id=claim.id,
   223→                from_status=from_status,
   224→                to_status=to_status,
   225→            )
   226→        )
   227→        db.session.flush()
   228→        logger.info(
   229→            "claim %s transitioned %s → %s",
   230→            claim.id,
   231→            from_status.value,
   232→            to_status.value,
   233→        )
   234→

    37→    1. Validate required fields and line-item values (raises 400 on failure).
    38→    2. Load Member — 404 if not found or soft-deleted.
    39→    3. Find active Policy covering date_of_service — 422 if none.
    40→    4. Load Provider — 404 if not found or soft-deleted.
    41→    5. Create Claim(status=submitted, review_type=auto) + LineItem rows.
    42→    6. Append ClaimStatusHistory(from_status=None, to_status=submitted).
    43→    7. flush() — assigns IDs without committing; engine owns the commit.
    44→    8. Call AdjudicationEngine().run(claim.id) — adjudicates and commits.
    45→    9. Return refreshed claim.
    46→
    47→    Args:
    48→        data: Raw input dict with keys:
    49→            - member_id (str, required)
    50→            - provider_id (str, required)
    51→            - date_of_service (str, ISO 8601 date, required)
    52→            - line_items (list, required, non-empty; each item must have
    53→              diagnosis_code, cpt_code, and billed_amount > 0)
    54→
    55→    Returns:
    56→        The refreshed Claim with its final adjudicated status.
    57→
    58→    Raises:
    59→        BadRequestError: If required fields are missing, date is unparseable,
    60→            line_items is empty, or any line item has billed_amount <= 0.
    61→        NotFoundError: If member_id or provider_id does not exist.
    62→        ValidationError: If no active policy covers the date of service.
    63→    """
    64→    member_id: str | None = data.get("member_id")
    65→    provider_id: str | None = data.get("provider_id")
    66→    date_of_service_str: str | None = data.get("date_of_service")
    67→    line_items_data = data.get("line_items")
    68→
    69→    # 1. Validate required top-level fields
    70→    if not member_id:
    71→        raise BadRequestError("member_id is required")
    72→    if not provider_id:
    73→        raise BadRequestError("provider_id is required")
    74→    if not date_of_service_str:
    75→        raise BadRequestError("date_of_service is required")
    76→    if line_items_data is None:
    77→        raise BadRequestError("line_items is required and must be a non-empty list")
    78→    if not isinstance(line_items_data, list) or len(line_items_data) == 0:
    79→        raise BadRequestError("line_items must be a non-empty list")
    80→
    81→    try:
    82→        dos = date.fromisoformat(str(date_of_service_str))
    83→    except ValueError as exc:
    84→        raise BadRequestError("date_of_service must be a valid ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD)") from exc
    85→
    86→    for item in line_items_data:
    87→        if not item.get("diagnosis_code"):
    88→            raise BadRequestError("Each line item must have a diagnosis_code")
    89→        if not item.get("cpt_code"):
    90→            raise BadRequestError("Each line item must have a cpt_code")
    91→        billed_raw = item.get("billed_amount")
    92→        if billed_raw is None:
    93→            raise BadRequestError("Each line item must have a billed_amount")
    94→        try:
    95→            billed = Decimal(str(billed_raw))
    96→        except InvalidOperation as exc:
    97→            raise BadRequestError("billed_amount must be a valid number") from exc
    98→        if billed <= Decimal("0"):
    99→            raise BadRequestError("billed_amount must be greater than 0")
   100→
   101→    # 2. Load Member
   102→    member: Member | None = db.session.execute(
   103→        select(Member).where(Member.id == member_id, Member.deleted_at.is_(None))
   104→    ).scalar_one_or_none()
   105→    if member is None:
   106→        raise NotFoundError(f"Member '{member_id}' not found")
   107→
   108→    # 3. Find active policy covering date of service
   109→    policy: Policy | None = db.session.execute(
   110→        select(Policy).where(
   111→            Policy.member_id == member_id,
   112→            Policy.status == PolicyStatus.active,
   113→            Policy.start_date <= dos,
   114→            Policy.end_date >= dos,
   115→            Policy.deleted_at.is_(None),
   116→        )
   117→    ).scalar_one_or_none()
   118→    if policy is None:
   119→        raise ValidationError("No active policy covers this date of service")
   120→
   121→    # 4. Load Provider
   122→    provider: Provider | None = db.session.execute(
   123→        select(Provider).where(Provider.id == provider_id, Provider.deleted_at.is_(None))
   124→    ).scalar_one_or_none()
   125→    if provider is None:
   126→        raise NotFoundError(f"Provider '{provider_id}' not found")
   127→
   128→    # 5. Create Claim (flush to get claim.id before adding related rows)
   129→    claim = Claim(
   130→        member_id=member_id,
   131→        policy_id=policy.id,
   132→        provider_id=provider_id,
   133→        date_of_service=dos,
   134→        status=ClaimStatus.submitted,
   135→        review_type=ReviewType.auto,
   136→    )
   137→    db.session.add(claim)
   138→    db.session.flush()
   139→
   140→    for item in line_items_data:
   141→        db.session.add(
   142→            LineItem(
   143→                claim_id=claim.id,
   144→                diagnosis_code=item["diagnosis_code"],
   145→                cpt_code=item["cpt_code"],
   146→                billed_amount=Decimal(str(item["billed_amount"])),
   147→            )
   148→        )
   149→
   150→    # 6. Append initial status history
   151→    db.session.add(
   152→        ClaimStatusHistory(
   153→            claim_id=claim.id,
   154→            from_status=None,
   155→            to_status=ClaimStatus.submitted,
   156→        )
   157→    )
   158→
   159→    # 7. Flush to assign IDs — engine owns the single commit
   160→    db.session.flush()
   161→
   162→    # 8. Run adjudication (engine commits the entire transaction)
   163→    AdjudicationEngine().run(claim.id)
   164→
   165→    # 9. Return refreshed claim
   166→    db.session.refresh(claim)
   167→    logger.info("claim %s submitted; final status=%s", claim.id, claim.status.value)
   168→    return claim
   169→

1→# Claims Processing System
     2→
     3→An insurance claims processing system that handles claim submission, coverage adjudication, lifecycle tracking, and dispute resolution.
     4→
     5→## Overview
     6→
     7→Members submit healthcare claims for reimbursement. The system evaluates each claim against the member's active policy, applies deductible and coinsurance rules per line item, and tracks the claim through its full lifecycle.
     8→
     9→**Key entities:**
    10→
    11→- **Member** — the insured person
    12→- **Provider** — healthcare professional or facility (identified by NPI)
    13→- **Plan** — template defining coverage rules, deductible, and coinsurance percentages
    14→- **Policy** — a member's active instance of a plan with a policy period
    15→- **Claim** — a reimbursement request containing one or more line items
    16→- **LineItem** — a single billed service (CPT procedure code, ICD-10 diagnosis code, billed amount)
    17→
    18→## Features
    19→
    20→- Claim submission with one or more line items
    21→- Line-item adjudication: deductible tracking + coinsurance calculation
    22→- Claim lifecycle state machine: `submitted` → `under_review` → `approved` / `denied` / `partially_approved` → `paid`
    23→- One-time dispute mechanism with re-adjudication
    24→- Human-readable explanation for every adjudication decision
    25→- Web UI: member dashboard, claim detail view, admin panel
    26→- REST API (`/api/*`)
    27→
    28→## Tech Stack
    29→
    30→| Layer | Technology |
    31→|---|---|
    32→| Language | Python 3.13 |
    33→| Backend API | Flask |
    34→| Database | SQLite |
    35→| ORM | Flask-SQLAlchemy |
    36→| Migrations | Flask-Migrate (Alembic) |
    37→| Frontend | Vanilla JavaScript |
    38→| Tooling | uv, ruff |
    39→
    40→## Project Structure
    41→
    42→```
    43→claims-processing-system/
    44→├── app/
    45→│   ├── __init__.py          # Flask app factory
    46→│   ├── models.py            # ORM models
    47→│   ├── extensions.py        # SQLAlchemy + Migrate setup
    48→│   ├── errors.py            # Custom errors & handlers
    49→│   ├── routes/              # API blueprints (members, providers, plans, policies, claims)
    50→│   ├── services/            # Business logic (adjudication, claims, disputes)
    51→│   └── static/              # Frontend (HTML, CSS, JS)
    52→├── tests/
    53→│   ├── unit/                # Unit tests for services
    54→│   └── integration/         # API integration tests
    55→├── config/
    56→│   └── settings.py          # Config classes
    57→├── migrations/              # Alembic migration files
    58→└── docs/                    # Domain concepts, acceptance criteria, decisions
    59→```
    60→
    61→## Prerequisites
    62→
    63→- Python 3.13+
    64→- [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) package manager
    65→
    66→## Setup & Run
    67→
    68→```bash
    69→# 1. Install dependencies (creates .venv automatically)
    70→uv sync
    71→
    72→# 2. Apply database migrations
    73→uv run flask --app app:create_app db upgrade
    74→
    75→# 3. Start the development server
    76→uv run flask --app app:create_app run
    77→```
    78→
    79→The app is available at **http://127.0.0.1:5000**.
    80→
    81→| Path | Description |
    82→|---|---|
    83→| `/` | Member dashboard — look up members and view their claims |
    84→| `/claim` | Claim detail — adjudication results and dispute submission |
    85→| `/admin` | Admin panel — manage plans, policies, and disputes |
    86→| `/api/*` | REST API |
    87→
    88→## Environment Variables
    89→
    90→| Variable | Default | Description |
    91→|---|---|---|
    92→| `DATABASE_URL` | `sqlite:///claims.db` | SQLite database file path |
    93→
    94→Create a `.env` file in the project root to override defaults:
    95→
    96→```
    97→DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///custom.db
    98→```
    99→
   100→## Running Tests
   101→
   102→```bash
   103→uv run pytest                    # all tests
   104→uv run pytest tests/unit/        # unit tests only
   105→uv run pytest tests/integration/ # integration tests only
   106→```
   107→
   108→## Code Quality
   109→
   110→```bash
   111→uv run ruff format .   # auto-format
   112→uv run ruff check .    # lint
   113→uv run mypy app/       # type check
   114→```
   115→
   116→## Documentation

   119→- [`docs/acceptance-criteria.md`](docs/acceptance-criteria.md) — functional requirements and scope

` placeholder - Loads `claim-detail.js` as a module script **Claim Detail Rendering (lines 209-224 in claim-detail.js):** ```javascript // Re-adjudication card (under_review + manual) if (claim.status === "under_review" && claim.review_type === "manual") { sections.push(`
Trigger Re-adjudication

ADMIN DASHBOARD LOCATION **Frontend Files:** - **HTML:** `/Users/harshit/Documents/Personal/claims-processing-system/app/static/admin.html` - **JavaScript:** `/Users/harshit/Documents/Personal/claims-processing-system/app/static/js/admin.js` **Route Handler:** `/Users/harshit/Documents/Personal/claims-processing-system/app/routes/pages.py` (lines 31-38) ```python @bp.route("/admin") def admin() -> Response: """Serve the admin panel page.""" return send_from_directory(current_app.static_folder, "admin.html") ``` **Admin Dashboard Features (from admin.html):** 1. **Plans Tab** - Create plans, manage coverage rules for CPT codes 2. **Providers Tab** - Add healthcare providers (individual/facility) 3. **Members Tab** - Create members with demographic info 4. **Policies Tab** - Assign plans to members for specific date ranges The admin panel does NOT directly show the re-adjudication form. Instead, admins would: - Go to the member dashboard (`/`) - Look up a member - Click on a claim to view its detail page - See the re-adjudication form if the claim is in `under_review` status with `manual` review type --- ## 3. DISPUTE TRACKING FLOW **Models:** `/Users/harshit/Documents/Personal/claims-processing-system/app/models.py` (lines 360-384) **Dispute Model:** ```python class Dispute(Base): """A member's challenge to a denied or partially-approved claim.""" id: Mapped[str] claim_id: Mapped[str] # FK to Claim (unique - one dispute per claim max) reason: Mapped[str] # Member's explanation reviewer_note: Mapped[str | None] # Admin/reviewer notes (filled during re-adjudication) submitted_at: Mapped[datetime] resolved_at: Mapped[datetime | None] status: Mapped[DisputeStatus] # pending or resolved deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None] ``` **DisputeStatus Enum:** `pending` → `resolved` ### Complete Dispute Submission Flow: **Step 1: Member Submits Dispute** - **Frontend Location:** `claim-detail.js` (lines 170-185) - **Condition:** Shown when claim status is `denied` or `partially_approved` AND no dispute exists - **API Call:** `api.submitDispute(claim.id, reason)` **Step 2: Backend Processing** - **Route:** `/api/claims//disputes` POST (claims.py, lines 180-195) - **Service:** `dispute_service.submit_dispute()` (/Users/harshit/Documents/Personal/claims-processing-system/app/services/dispute_service.py, lines 23-77) **Submit Dispute Service Logic:** ```python def submit_dispute(claim_id: str, reason: str) -> Dispute: # Validate claim exists and is deniable (denied or partially_approved) claim = db.session.get(Claim, claim_id) if claim.status not in (ClaimStatus.denied, ClaimStatus.partially_approved): raise ConflictError(...) # Ensure no duplicate dispute if claim.dispute is not None: raise ConflictError("This claim has already been disputed...") # Transition claim from denied/partially_approved → under_review (manual) old_status = claim.status claim.status = ClaimStatus.under_review claim.review_type = ReviewType.manual # Create Dispute with status=pending dispute = Dispute(claim_id=claim.id, reason=reason) db.session.add(dispute) db.session.commit() return dispute ``` --- ## 4. RE-ADJUDICATION FLOW (Dispute → Form Display) ### Complete State Transition: ``` 1. Claim Status: denied/partially_approved ↓ 2. Member submits dispute - Claim → under_review (review_type: manual) - Dispute created with status=pending ↓ 3. Re-adjudication form displayed (in claim-detail.js, lines 209-224) - Shown because: status === "under_review" && review_type === "manual" ↓ 4. Admin/Reviewer triggers re-adjudication - API: POST /api/claims//adjudicate ↓ 5. Backend Processing (dispute_service.trigger_readjudication): - Mark dispute as resolved (status=resolved, resolved_at=now) - Delegate to AdjudicationEngine.run(claim.id) ↓ 6. Adjudication Engine Processes (adjudication_engine.py): - Re-evaluates all line items against plan coverage rules - Creates new AdjudicationResult with revision+1 - Updates claim status based on results: * All approved → approved → paid * All denied → denied * Mix → partially_approved * If partially_approved AND dispute resolved → paid (auto-payment) - Creates Payment record if transitioning to paid ↓ 7. Claim Final Status with Payment ``` ### Re-adjudication Trigger Handler: **Frontend (claim-detail.js, lines 262-278):** ```javascript const readjBtn = document.getElementById("readjudicate-btn"); if (readjBtn) { readjBtn.addEventListener("click", async () => { const note = document.getElementById("reviewer-note").value.trim() || null; const errEl = document.getElementById("readjudicate-error"); readjBtn.disabled = true; readjBtn.textContent = "Processing…"; try { await api.adjudicate(claim.id, note); // POST /api/claims//adjudicate loadClaim(); // Refresh claim to show new status } catch (err) { showError(errEl, err.message); readjBtn.disabled = false; readjBtn.textContent = "Trigger Re-adjudication"; } }); } ``` **Backend Route (claims.py, lines 218-231):** ```python @bp.route("//adjudicate", methods=["POST"]) def trigger_readjudication(claim_id: str) -> Response: """Trigger manual re-adjudication of a disputed claim.""" data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {} reviewer_note: str | None = data.get("reviewer_note") claim = dispute_service.trigger_readjudication(claim_id, reviewer_note) return jsonify(_serialize_claim(claim)) ``` **Service Handler (dispute_service.py, lines 80-126):** ```python def trigger_readjudication(claim_id: str, reviewer_note: str | None) -> Claim: """Resolve dispute and trigger re-adjudication.""" claim = db.session.get(Claim, claim_id) if claim.status != ClaimStatus.under_review or claim.review_type != ReviewType.manual: raise ConflictError("Claim is not awaiting manual review") dispute = claim.dispute if dispute.status != DisputeStatus.pending: raise ConflictError("Dispute is already resolved") # Mark dispute as resolved if reviewer_note is not None: dispute.reviewer_note = reviewer_note dispute.status = DisputeStatus.resolved dispute.resolved_at = datetime.utcnow() db.session.flush() # Delegate to adjudication engine AdjudicationEngine().run(claim.id) db.session.refresh(claim) return claim ``` --- ## 5. RELATED API ENDPOINTS **All dispute/adjudication endpoints in `/api/claims`:** | Method | Endpoint | Handler | Purpose | |--------|----------|---------|---------| | POST | `//disputes` | `submit_dispute()` | Member submits dispute | | GET | `//dispute` | `get_dispute()` | Retrieve dispute details | | POST | `//adjudicate` | `trigger_readjudication()` | Admin triggers re-adjudication | | POST | `//accept` | `accept_payment()` | Member accepts partial payment (no dispute) | | GET | `//payment` | `get_payment()` | Retrieve payment record | --- ## 6. KEY DATA FLOW SUMMARY ### Dispute Submission: **Member Dashboard (`/`) → Claim Detail (`/claim?id=`) → Submit Dispute Button → API → Dispute Created → Claim under_review** ### Re-adjudication Form Display: **Happens automatically when claim status = `under_review` AND review_type = `manual`** - Rendered by `claim-detail.js:renderActionSections()` - Shows "Trigger Re-adjudication" card (lines 209-224) ### Re-adjudication Execution: **Reviewer fills optional note → Trigger Re-adjudication → Backend processes → Claim gets final status → Payment created if approved** ### File Locations Summary: - **Dispute Model:** `/app/models.py` (lines 360-384) - **Dispute Service:** `/app/services/dispute_service.py` - **Adjudication Engine:** `/app/services/adjudication_engine.py` - **Claims Routes:** `/app/routes/claims.py` - **Claim Detail Frontend:** `/app/static/js/claim-detail.js` - **Claim Detail Page:** `/app/static/claim.html` - **Admin Dashboard:** `/app/static/admin.html` + `/app/static/js/admin.js` - **Page Routes:** `/app/routes/pages.py`
